entry.title: Re: Error Message Type 'W' gets handled as 'I' in BADI
entry.content:
Hi Filip,

Yes, I think it is possible that some BAdI just runs like this because the value of type is correct.

Best Regards,

Zhehui

entry.title: Re: WM putaway manual bin selection
entry.content:
Hi 

Manual Strategy in WM has below effects 

    - Manual stock placement

  • No strategy is used.
  • During transfer order processing, for placement into a storage type with manual stock placement, the storage bin will be selected and entered manually by the user.
  • Recommended for interim storage types.
  • Can also be used in conjunction with Storage Unit Management.
  • Since no strategy is used, you leave the Putaway strategy field blank in the storage type record.
  • See below sap help
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/b2dee5e83e2446149294f9860a7c08f0/a390c95360267214e10000000a174cb4.html

If it does not work as per your expectation so Please share your config and test data screen shot

 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Re: Differences between S/4HANA Cloud PrivateEdition and...
entry.content:
Hi 

See below thread 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/sap-public-vs-private-cloud-understanding-the-differences/ba-p/13572474

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/sap-public-vs-private-cloud-understanding-the-differences/ba-p/13572474

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Re: S4 Upgrade: Starting the Shadow Instance after a reboot
entry.content:
Hello,

I'd check the message server trace file and the work process ones..ç
If the work processes don't start the dispatcher dies.

kind regards,
Mercedes

entry.title: Re: MB1C is obsolete now. Now which T-code helps us to ad...
entry.content:
Hi 

MB1C is no more with S/4 hana. you can use MIGO transaction. Also there MIGO_GR for external Procurement and MIGO_GO , 

Check this I think it will work

 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Inventory Aging Report SAP B1 (HANA)
entry.content:
Hi. I would like to know if there is any way we can get information regarding View, Table, or store procedures that SAP is using to execute SAP default reports like Inventory Aging.

My requirement is to duplicate the SAP B1 Inventory Aging Report along with some additional information.
I tried multiple ways but was unable to duplicate the same as the SAP Report.

Any help in this matter is highly appreciated.

entry.title: ENCODE_FOR_URL problem with Slash Character "/"
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m using the ENCODE_FOR_URL function in an “Update Record” node, especially at field “WorkPackageName” as follows:

In the example, as you can see, everything looks to work properly by replacing both “spaces” with “%20” & “slashes” with “%2F” in my original text: “Senior Java Developer / Scrummaster (WS)”.

However, while accessing this code block from the deployed app I’m getting the following link:

https://hostXXX.launchpad.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/buildapps87351.buildapps87351-3.1.3/destinations/S4HC_PROJECT_ODATA_CREATE_UPDATE_1/WorkPackageSet(ProjectID='C000138M',WorkPackageID='C000138M.1.2',WorkPackageName='Senior%20Java%20Developer%20/%20Scrummaster%20(WS)')?%24format=json

As you may see “spaces” have been successfully translated, though the “slash” still remains in its original state as character: WorkPackageName='Senior%20Java%20Developer%20/%20Scrummaster%20(WS)'.

This subsequently causes me error on updating Work Packages with “Slashes” on this description field.

Do you have any clue why ENCODE_FOR_URL translates selectively special chars?

Many thanks in advance!

Thanos

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 Hana Query with Date parameter in Variable
entry.content: That is simplest way. But if query is complex or has subqueries, then direct assigning value to parameter will not work. Parameter has to be assigned in query then can be linked to value later.

Can i disable this message or give permanent instruction to set yes always.
SAP B! version of HANA 10.0

Harry Splinter

entry.title: SAP IBP objects not transported via export/import functionality
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am trying to use the export/import functionality between a dev and a productive IBP tenants and I face an issue with Analytics charts and Planner Workspace content (workspaces and workbooks). I am not able to transport them at all. On the other hand, dashboards and all other objects are transported without any issue. The planning area is a copy of SAPIBP1 unified planning area.

This is the result of my check option:

P.S.: It is strange that 7 charts got warning messages here, while in fact the available charts are a lot more. 

Any recommendation would be appreciated. Thank you in advance.

BR,
Yuliyan

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Central Business Configuration 2409 FD2
entry.content:
With the latest SAP Central Business Configuration 2409 FD2 release, an exciting enhancement is now available. This blog post will provide you with a detailed overview of this enhancement.

Enhanced Notifications

Notifications in Project Experience are prominently displayed and easily accessible via the bell icon, ensuring users stay informed and up to date. The enhanced notifications are now displayed for all projects assigned to a user in a structured format. This eliminates the need to switch between projects to view project-specific notifications.

The notifications will remain visible until they are dismissed. Users can conveniently view notifications organized by project, notification type, or date. Additionally, users with the SAP_CBC_CONSUMPTION_PROGRAM_LEAD role can access notifications for all projects.

Note: Please be informed that the notifications are visible only if the email address is maintained for the business users.

Bell Icon with notifications

 For a quick summary, check out this video about  What's New in 2409 FD2. Don’t forget to also use the What’s New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal, which provides further details and links about all the recent changes.

That wraps up the new features this time. Make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

Do you have an idea on how to further enhance SAP Central Business Configuration? Go to the SAP Customer Influence portal to submit an improvement request or vote for the ideas of others. This helps us understand your requirements even better and prioritize our work accordingly. We’re looking forward to your improvement requests.

entry.title: Re: BR0100E Internal error for 'backup_status: at locatio...
entry.content:
Hi Amal,

BRBACKUP and so BR*Tools is working fine.

There is a problem with the data file '/oracle/SID/sapdata2/sr3_127/sr3.data127'. 

Here is what to do:

1.) Check if the file exists on the disk. You can check this e.g. with "ls -al" command

2.) If the file exists, check if the file is in a correct status and it is online
SQL> select FILE#,NAME,STATUS from v$datafile where name like '%sr3.data127%';
SQL> select FILE_ID,FILE_NAME,STATUS from dba_data_files where FILE_NAME like '%sr3.data127%';

3.) Perform the necessary changes to bring the file ONLINE.

In case the file is not present on the disk, use a backup and recover the datafile.

Regards,

János

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufacturing | Manage Custom Data
entry.content:

Sunny

entry.title: Re: Hit error when applying patch 19.24.0.0.240716 - 202408 on RedHat 8
entry.content:
Hi, as the error indicates, the error happens with line 4. This is as below:
...
'13627489:ON','13699643:OFF','14255600:ON','14595273:ON','18405517:2','20355502:10',
...
Looks like one of these fixes is not present on the system. Are you sure that the SBP installation was successful without any error?

entry.title: Wrong data type identified in calculations
entry.content:
When creating new calculations with the FX transformation, in many cases DSP seems to get ‘lost’ in identifying the data type derived from the calculation.
For example the expression

CASE WHEN EMAIL IS NULL THEN true ELSE false END

is correctly identified as a BOOLEAN type.

But with a small modification

CASE WHEN IFNULL(EMAIL, '') = '' THEN true ELSE false END

It gets messed up and says it returns a STRING, which is incorrect.

What do you do in these cases?
Do you force the conversion with something like

TO_BOOLEAN(CASE WHEN IFNULL(EMAIL, '') = '' THEN true ELSE false END)

?

I'm not sure about the processing impact of having lots of conversion functions (or LEFTs, etc) that are not necessary, but if we don't put them when validating DSP returns the wrong type of data, maybe the HANA optimizer discards them?

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Workforce replication from SAP Successfactors to SAP S/4HANA Cloud using SAP Master Data Integra

entry.title: Use case to demonstrate AIF Structure Persistence within SAP S/4
entry.content:
Automate the goods movement posting when a freight document is created.

  • In addition, they want a mechanism to view, monitor, edit and reprocess the failed goods movement messages.
  • As-Is Challenge: There are multiple avenues in SAP to automate the goods movement posting For e.g. It can be done via enhancements, output processing etc. But tracking, monitoring and error recovery of such postings have always been a challenge.
  • Solution Approach: This blog highlights how AIF structure persistence can be leveraged to overcome these challenges.
  • AIF Overview: Application Interface Framework (AIF) - SAP delivered solution that helps users manage, monitor application interfaces in the system. Using the AIF runtime environment, you can trigger AIF messages directly from enhancements, customer-specific reports etc. Business/ Technical users responsible for the posting can track the status using the AIF Message Monitoring Fiori app or from the GUI using /AIF/ERR
  • It is also possible correct errors, restart, or cancel messages.
  • Component Details:
  • AIF is available in SAP S/4 HANA on-premise edition.
  • Detailed configurations Steps are provided below.
  • 1  Interface Development - Configures the AIF message and its Action for goods movement posting
  • 1.1.   Create DDIC T-Code SE11
  • 1.2.   Define Namespace T-Code /AIF/CUST
  • 1.3.   Define Interfaces T-Code /AIF/CUST
  • 1.4.   Specify Interface engine T-Code /AIF/CUST
  • 1.5.   Run Program /AIF/PERS_TABLE_GENERATOR T-Code SE38
  • 1.6.   Define Actions T-Code /AIF/CUST
  • 1.7.   Define Structure Mapping T-Code /AIF/CUST

2.  Error Handling- Configures the Fiori Message Monitoring Tile to monitor, edit and reprocess the message

1.8.   Define Namespace Specific Features T-Code /AIF/CUST

1.8.1.   Assign Fiori Tile Name

1.8.2.   Create Recipient

1.9.   Define Interface Specific Features T-Code /AIF/CUST

1.9.1.   Assign Search Fields based on the tables generated in step 1.5

1.9.2.   Assign Recipient created in step 1.8.2 

3. System Configuration - Assign the message monitoring tile to the user

1.10 Assign users to AIF recipient created in step 1.8.2 T-Code /AIF/CUST

4. Runtime Configuration

1.11 Activate Runtime Configuration for the namespace created in step 1.2 T-Code  AIF/PERS_CGR

5. triggering AIF Messages to post Goods Movement

1.12 Trigger AIF message using class /AIF/CL_ENABLER_XML=>TRANSFER_TO_AIF

  use the DDIC structure created in step 1.1

1.1.    Create DDIC T-Code SE11

Create SAP DATA Structure

Create Raw DATA Structure 

1.2.    Define Namespace T-Code /AIF/CUST

Navigate to the below path and Create a New Namespace

1.3.    Define Interfaces T-Code /AIF/CUST

Navigate to the below path, provide the namespace created in the previous step and Create a New Interface

Provide the SAP Data structure and raw data structure created in the initial steps

1.4.    Specify Interface engine T-Code /AIF/CUST

Navigate to the below path

Select the Namespace

Select the Interface and provide the structure persistence engines

1.5.    Run Program /AIF/PERS_TABLE_GENERATOR in SE38.

Provide the Namespace, Interface Name, Version, package, prefix and request. The Source Structure will be picked from the Interface configuration

Click on Execute

Structure Persistence tables are generated - note them down for use in the configuration

1.6.    Define Actions T-Code /AIF/CUST - Define Action -> Select Interface

Click New Entry

Give an action name and other details as specified below

For the action define the function

Create the function by forward navigation

In the action function module read the data in the AIF structures and post the BAPI to create goods movement

1.7.    Define Structure Mapping T-Code /AIF/CUST

Go to Structure Mapping - >Navigate to the interface

 

Navigate to Assign Actions and click on new entry

Give the action name created in step 1.6

1.18 Define Namespace Specific Features 

Select interface-specific features on the left pan and click on New Entries

Give the interface display name and select display descriptions

On the left pane navigate to define recipients and click new entries and give a recipient name

1.19.    Define Interface Specific Features

Create Key Fields Required, for this Demo Created 2 Key Fields from ZROOT and 2 Key field from ZGM_ITEM ( These tables were generated in 1.5 ) 

Navigate to Assign Recipient and enter the name created in step 1.8

1.10 Assign Users to Recipient (System Configuration)

 

After the above config - > Navigate to Fiori Launch pad open the message monitoring app and you should be able to see the new tile created as below.

1.11 Define Runtime Configuration

Go to T-Code /AIF/PERS_CGR and Enter the Namespace

In the Next Screen Activate the Runtime Config

1.12 Trigger Goods Movement Posting

Insert the below code in enhancement / Custom code / Output Processing Routine from wherever goods movement needs to be triggered

Conclusion:

After the above AIF configuration whenever the AIF message is triggered the user would be to view them in the Message Monitoring Fiori App or /AIF/ERR GUI T-code

The user can monitor, restart, cancel the AIF messages. AIF provides better visibility to both the business users and technical users

Message monitoring app

In the next blog we will discuss on SAP TM Post Processing Framework to trigger AIF Messages.

entry.title: Customizing Program SAPF124 for Automatic Clearing of Open Items
entry.content:
Customizing SAPF124 for Automatic Clearing of Open Items

1. Overview of SAPF124
  • It automatically clears open items from customer, vendor, and G/L accounts (particularly GR/IR clearing accounts). It selects all accounts specified in the value sets with debit and credit postings.
  • For the final clearing of the open items, SAPF124 makes a CALL TRANSACTION ‘FB1S’ (G/L Account) or ‘FB1D’ (Customer) or ‘FB1K’ (Vendor), thus avoiding double coding for the clearing itself. 
2. Key objects of SAPF124
  • The main internal table for item display is GT_DLIST;
  • Include F124_DETAIL_LIST will fill the item table and output by writing the list;
  • The key routine Routine for account data populating is ACC_APPEND_DATA;


  • Include F124_MERGE, which contains logic for selecting tables from the main table: BSID/BSIK/BSIS and further data consolidation.
3. Tips when customizing SAPF124

3.1 About table TF123(Additional Rules for Running Program SAPF124)

SAPF124 selects all accounts specified in the value sets with debit and credit postings according to customizing for SAP table TF123 via SAP transaction OB74. 

The defaulted Clearing Criteria/Rule for Additional Rules For Automatic Clearing are:

  • Criterion 1 = Technical Field ZUONR for Assignment Number.

  • Criterion 2 = Technical Field GSBER for Business Area.

  • Criterion 3 = Technical Field VBUND for Trading Partner. 

Be cautious that the criteria field from BEDG1 to BEDG5 has a limit of 30 characters. For example, trying to use Item Text (BSEG-SGTXT) as one of the criteria will lead to incorrect clearing because the SGTXT is 50 characters long.

Besides, the definition of the criteria field BEDGX only supports 24 characters long less than 30 of TF123!

To support longer fields like SGTXT as criteria then have to customize the following places for SAPF124 (better create Z one and its include program):

  1. Create a new Z table ZTF123 refer to TF123 and extend the length of BEDG fields from 30 to 50;
  2. Update the length for the definition of field BEDG from 24 to 50 at types like ys_xbsis/ ys_dlist/ xbsidgr/ xbsik, etc;
  3. Recalculate the position of criteria fields after the length extension, and update all related write-list routines like Record_document_neu/RECORD_DOCUMENT_LG. 

Check every place criteria field has been used including definition/populating/displaying. Dozens of places need to be adjusted after this length extension. May take some time but it will work.

3.2 About archived documents during clearing items

SAPF124 will encounter a termination popup (with Error F1 800-Clearing: Document & & & does not exist) while trying to post any archived documents and terminate the whole process.

We can use BSIS-XARCH as an archived indicator to separate from the normal FI documents.

Remember to add an XARCH check for all data fetching toward table BSID/BSIS/BSIK.

Related notes:

196118 - Automatic clearing with tolerance - SAPF124

Automatic Clearing of Open Items F.13 (SAPF124 and SAPF124E)
972992 - SAPF124: Heading truncated

entry.title: Re: Partitioning ORACLE
entry.content:
Hi,
As it is documented in SAP ntoe ## 722188 - FAQ: Oracle partitioning, as of 01.12.2019, partitioning additional objects in OLTP systems is no longer an option. 
Please check this important info from above SAP note:
...

As of December 01, 2019, we will no longer support the use of the Partitioning Engine for SAP/Oracle systems. Customers who have been using the SAP Partitioning Engine in productive SAP systems before December 01, 2019 will continue to receive support.

Important: You should not partition tables as a way of improve query performance in an OLTP system; you must apply partitioning very carefully and after doing a detailed analysis so as to not negatively affect runtime performance. Partition elimination can be useful if there are full scans of large objects, as it allows  to avoid full scanning large pieces of an object. Partitioning can still offer the other benefits (administration, higher avalability, contention reduction) keeping in mind that the SAP OLTP standard applications are not aware of special partition operations (like dropping a partition) Partitioning increases the size of the Oracle Data Dictionary which may slow down queries accessing data dictionary tables such as dba_segments or dba_extents.


...
However, BW objects in an OLAP system can be partitioned, by partitioning on the application level.

entry.title: Re: “ABAP AI is our Shared Treasure” - ABAP AI is on its way.
entry.content:
Is abap AI available for onPremise versions or it’s only for cloud versions?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Personas - Admin question or rather your best pra...
entry.content:
Yes, but if this is your regular process, then enabling push to client would be the best suited solution. It just needs a simple setting, and it avoids having to use transports from 210 to 200.

In fact, if your primary development client is 210 and you don't make changes to your objects elsewhere, then you don't even need to change the original client in 200. You can just transport from there to all other systems.

entry.title: Re: WM putaway manual bin selection
entry.content:
Hi

I have few question. Why you are using use exit in put away strategy? I dont think it should be ticked 

What is the bin which is coming by default If it is 921 so it is interim bin it will come automatically. and I dont see any storage location change in your TO screen shot.

Also Please check if you have maintained putaway indicator in material master warehouse management view. if it is there please remove.

It is simple strategy it does should bring bin.

Check and let me know 

Regards

Rashid Khan

Margit

Could this be any different ports for Agent Listener , Agent Admin or any thing other?

Kindly suggest.thanks in Advance..

Regards,

Ashish

entry.title: Re: Implementation of Time Valuation on Commenced Half Hour
entry.content:
Hello Nicklas,

can you explain why you did not use the business rules in time valuation? Our engineering state that above is achievalbe with a calculation rule - without custom MDF, without lookup.
Note that on save rules on the time sheet are very performance critical.

You can also write me on private note if you want.

Best regards

Volker

Volker

entry.title: Re: Public Dimension Table Import Job Error
entry.content: Hi, the SAP note indicates that the issue has been fixed and deployed as part of the patch 2024.21.7.We are on 2024.21.8 version and still facing this issue.

entry.title: Re: WM putaway manual bin selection
entry.content: Hi, no putaway indicator set. It gets moved from 921 UML-Zone to Storage bin last used. Maybe i got your question wrong. Posting was Non WM location 311 to WM location (via MIGO). Triggering a complete TO to last used bin (while expecting TR to be stuck in LB10 because somebody needs to add the bin. The User-Exit mark is set because its needed at the GI for set creation. No coding for receiving TO.

entry.title: Re: Automate Email Attachment Extraction Using SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Re: Update G/L Accounts according to new UDF data?
entry.content:
Hi,

if it would be possible, there were a checkbox to remember the answer.

regards Lothar

entry.title: handling of additional information regarding terms of payment
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I have a question regarding payment terms:

in our company we are having the issue that payment terms are sometimes duplicated, because the business colleagues are requesting the same payment terms but with different texts. e. G. the payment term "to be paid within 30 days"  needs to be created several times, because there are texts requested like “to be paid by check”, “with bank guarantee”, or  “to be paid after delivery”.

Currently, our internal SAP team doesn´t set up payment terms with the same parameters but with different texts. They say usually  “additional info regarding payment terms need to be updated in texts fields in the PO or in the sales document”

After investigating, I couldn´t find out which texts fields can be used for this.
In case of a purchase order, in my opinion, it needs to be a texts field which is also shown in MIRO when the invoice is posted, to make sure that the FI document is handled correctly. In SD we would need some kind of process to maintain this data in the FI document, otherwise the info is missing for dunning.

How can this additional information for payment terms be handled in the system? Is there a standard way in SAP to handle this?

many thanks,

entry.title: Re: New Videos on DMS with SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
@Jakub_Roguski i should hire you as my publicist 🤗

more videos to come, including how to integrate DMS in automations with relatively new activities and even a bonus one on connecting DMS to S/4HANA

entry.title: custom filter not working
entry.content:
Hi

Hoping someone can help please. I've created a custom formula below in the Calculation Editor as a Calculated Dimension. This checks to see if a course has been used in the last 365 days and if it has put a 1 in the field and if not put a 0. This works perfectly so no issues with this part. I am able to add this up to see how many courses have been used within a year. But for some reason I am unable to use this as a filter on the page. So from a cross-tab list of all the courses I want to filter on those courses used within a year. I have a simple bar chart showing this volume. I set it as a Linked Analysis to filter all widgets on the page. But it simply doesn't do anything when I click on it. Am I doing anything wrong or can you not use a custom formula as a filter? 

IF([#Most Recent Activity]<=365,1,0)

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP UI5 Reuse Change Document Component OData V4
entry.content: Did you find a solution, that works for you? If yes, would you share your solution? We have a similar requirement (reuse of attachment service component which is v2 and we have a v4 service) and can not get it working.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps for SAP Business One, the next-gen Development Environment
entry.content:
Hi @Tâm_Nguyễn,

About SAP Process Automation, I recommend the following enablement:

Step-by-step videos

1 - Supplier Invoice Upload for Intelligent Invoice Scanning (2R1 template)

2 - Business Document Extraction from E-Mail (2X1 template)

Best regards,
Darius

entry.title: Re: Public Dimension Table Import Job Error
entry.content: As stated in SAP Note 3538935, the issue has been fixed with the new patch.

entry.title: Re: Form template rule - parameter settings in ByD
entry.content: Hi

entry.title: Re: Form template rule - parameter settings in ByD
entry.content: How to add Parameter settings: Sales Unit? I cant find the way. thank you for help. Magda

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 Hana Query with Date parameter in Variable
entry.content:
It is important that SAP can recognize the parameters [%X] and understand which field this parameter belongs to. This can be done with the help of a small SQL query as a comment at the beginning of the query. You can then use the [%X] parameters in the query text because SAP already knows what this parameter means thanks to the comment line.

Your example can also work like this. It is not necessary to declare variables.

/* Select * From OINV T0 Where T0."DocDate" = [%0] */
Select "DocNum" From OINV Where "DocDate" = [%0];

But of course it can also work like this:

/* Select * From OINV T0 Where T0."DocDate" = [%0] */
Declare DateF Date;
DateF := [%0];
Select "DocNum" From OINV Where "DocDate" = DateF;

It is important to have a comment line at the beginning of the query and that the table gets an alias (for example T0) that is also used for the field that must be assigned to the parameter. 

/* SELECT * FROM OINV T0 WHERE T0."DocDate" = [%0] */

This way, SAP recognizes that the parameter [%0] means "booking date" and is assigned to the OINV.DocDate field. After that, it doesn't matter how complicated the query is, you can always use the parameter [%0] without having to define a variable.

entry.title: Re: Delivery for subcontracting process Mov 631
entry.content:
So you are using movement Type 631 for issue Goods to Subcontracting Vendor? 

Normally Movement Type 541 is used for Subcontracting. 

May be the below link is useful:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/subcontracting-movement-types/qaq-p/3266122

entry.title: How to Debug Existing Reports in Jaspersoft Studio for SAP Field Service Management?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m currently working on fixing errors in a report created by someone else in Jaspersoft Studio for SAP Field Service Management (FSM). While troubleshooting, I’ve encountered challenges in identifying and resolving the issues in the report output.

A major difficulty is that the error encountered when generating the report in SAP FSM does not appear in Jaspersoft Studio during testing. This discrepancy makes it harder to pinpoint the source of the issue and effectively resolve it.

Please note that I’m not designing a new report but focusing on debugging an existing one. Additionally, I’m not using any Java applications; I’m working exclusively with Jaspersoft Studio and SAP FSM.

I would greatly appreciate it if anyone could share their tips, best practices, or tools for debugging reports in this context. Are there specific logs, settings, or workflows you recommend for efficiently identifying and fixing issues, especially in cases where errors occur only in the FSM environment?

Thank you in advance for your support and advice!

Best regards,

entry.title: Only allow ‘New’ status to be selected when creating a service call in SAP FSM
entry.content:
Hello,

I want to create a validation expression in screen configuration to allow ‘New’ status to be selected when creating a service call.

Can anyone help me? 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: How can we consume northwind and S4HANA on premise se...
entry.content:
Hi bharatb1,

You should be able to use the "Service Manager" from Fiori tools to add more services to your application.  Please launch the command palette entry: "Fiori: Open Service Manager" and you should be able to select multiple services.

You can also see this blog for more details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/adding-multiple-odata-services-and-syncing-the-metadata-and-annotation-file/ba-p/13519193

Regards,

Ian

entry.title: Re: SAP Tech Bytes: Use Destinations During Local Development - Cloud Foundry Basics #4
entry.content: Hi @AdilBasha1SAP, these links work for me. Not sure why you can't access them. Maybe try another browser or a private window.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Enabling/Disabling DateTimePicker Fields B...
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I managed to solve the issue by preventing manual keyboard input in the DateTimePicker fields, ensuring users can only change the values using the DateTimePicker popup. Here's how I did it:

1. Add a custom CSS class to the DateTimePicker fields:
I added a class 'noKeyboardInput' to the DateTimePicker fields in the fragment. Here's an example:

<DateTimePicker
placeholder="YYYY/MM/DD HH:MM:SS"
width="450px" class="sapUiLargeMarginBottom noKeyboardInput"
displayFormat="yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"
value="{selectedRow>/LastLogin}"
valueFormat="yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"
enabled="{parts: ['selectedRow>/LastLogout', 'selectedRow>/LastLogin'], formatter: '.isLoginEnabled'}" />

2. Add the following CSS in the 'style.css' file:
The CSS disables keyboard interactions without affecting the popup:

.noKeyboardInput input {
pointer-events: none;
}

With this approach, users can not type values into the DateTimePicker fields but can still select values using the popup

I hope this helps anyone facing a similar issue. Feel free to reach out if you have any questions!

entry.title: Labelling length of dimensions in the bar chart
entry.content:
In a table: I can change the label length from short to medium length.

I can't find this option in the bar chart. Is it not possible to adjust the label length in the bar chart?

Short and medium-length text is maintained in the source system.

entry.title: Re: Absence Counting Rules 2.0 in Time Management - 2H 2023
entry.content:
Hi @priyanka 

Thanks for getting back! I understand the time records filter to be used in time tracking for time valuations and payments. (Could be wrong here too!)

This requirement is purely for absence counting deducting off a quota, and the client doesn't have time tracking or capture attendances.

I'm just wanting to change the deduction quantity of leave in the counting rule, on a public holiday (which I can do) or a night shift working in to a public holiday (which we cannot do outright). I have a work around for now but wanted to check on this.

This would help a lot, I am wondering what other clients do with the similar requirements if they don't use time tracking/ time records filtering.

Do you think it's worth logging a new feature request?

Kind regards

Shalene

entry.title: SAP CAPM service failing with http 407 Proxy Authentication failed Error
entry.content:
I am consuming a SOAP based web service from a S/4HANA On-prem system inside a SAP CAP service using sap-cloud-sdk api's coupled with BTP destinations and cloud connector. 

Reference :https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/consuming-soap-web-services-in-sap-cloud-application-programming-model-cap/ba-p/13522820

I have recently upgraded the @SAP-cloud-sdk/connectivity version to 3.23.0(latest) recently and now the service is failing sometimes with http status code 407- Proxy Authentication failed. The service works most of the time but randomly fails with this error. And once i redeploy the CAP app to CF again the service starts working like usual.

entry.title: Re: BAPI to change work center in mass
entry.content:
Hi @mohammedrahman4 ,

The below mentioned Functional Modules will be helpful while changing the work centers:

  1. CR_WORKCENTER_UPDATE
  2. CRAP_WORKCENTER_CHANGE
  3. CR_CAPACITIES_OF_WORKCENTER

You can select and work with the FM that would best suit your requirement.

Regards,
Ritesh PR

entry.title: Re: Public Dimension Table Import Job Error
entry.content:
Hello Bibi,

Yes, it fixed for us. Maybe you must check your import job.

entry.title: Re: Implementation of Time Valuation on Commenced Half Hour
entry.content:
Hello Volker,

I do believe that a Calculation Rule could perform the same, though worth noting in that case would be that the rounding would have to be performed a little differently, as we are dealing with a sum in minutes. And yes, it is of course also worth noting that the rule can be performance impacting, which is an important addendum.

I did not use a Calculation Rule due to some different factors. Firstly, a simple time aspect. We faced this demand in the 2024H1 release, where Business Rules in Time Valuation was not generally available. A retrofit could of course be beneficial rather than a custom solution post 2024H2, but that brings me naturally to me second point. The client I presented this solution to, much preferred a "table" to maintain rather than a Business Rule, as a table was more familiar to them. Thirdly, multiple scenarios that could be covered in the new release with either Decision Rules or Calculation Rules was found, and with a maximum of 5 valuations with business rules per Time Recording Profile, we opted to maintain the custom solution.

I hope that answers your questions - and thank you for reading the blog in the first place!

Kind regards,

Nicklas

entry.title: Re: WM putaway manual bin selection
entry.content: any setting in warehouse management view 2 please check also check have defined in any bin in wm movement type for storage location transfer or check

entry.title: Scenario : Company give mobile to the employee worth of Rs:100,000 and paid directly to the vendor,

entry.title: Hits from other Reporting Entities in Tax Compliance within Run Compliance Reports
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We are facing an issue with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) in the "Run Compliance Report" app, specifically in the context of tax compliance, and would appreciate your insights.

Scenario:
When a user runs a compliance report for a specific reporting entity (e.g., XYZ1), the system is also returning hits in tax compliance for other entities (XYZ2, XYZ3), where the user has authorizations.

Expectation:
Our client wants the system to return hits only for the selected reporting entity in Tax Compliance. For example, when the run compliance Reports app is executed for XYZ1, only hits for XYZ1 should appear in tax compliance, regardless of whether the user has authorizations for XYZ2 and XYZ3.

Key Details:

  • The user running the report has authorization for all three entities: XYZ1, XYZ2, and XYZ3.
  • The issue occurs when the report is executed with XYZ1 as the selected reporting entity in the app.
  • Our goal is to restrict the tax compliance hits to the selected reporting entity (XYZ1), without impacting the user's overall authorizations for the other entities.

    Additional Considerations:

    1. Compliance Scenarios and Compliance Checks:
      • In our configuration, compliance scenarios and compliance checks include selection conditions (e.g., equal to or not equal to company code, document type, tax code, etc.).
      • Could these selection conditions be impacting the results by allowing hits for other reporting entities (e.g., XYZ2 and XYZ3)? If so, what adjustments can we make to ensure results are strictly filtered for the selected reporting entity?

Question:

Is it possible to configure the "Run Compliance Report" app or adjust the logic in SAP DRC to ensure that tax compliance results are limited to the selected reporting entity (XYZ1) even if the user has authorization to multiple entities? Additionally, how can we ensure that the selection conditions in compliance scenarios and compliance checks do not inadvertently include results from other entities?

Additional Context:
We’ve reviewed the configuration settings for the app and considered authorization objects, but the issue persists. Could there be specific filters, configurations, or overlooked areas in compliance scenarios that need adjustments?

Thank you and Best regards

Adrian

entry.title: Re: Extend Datasphere Business content package.
entry.content:
Thanks @Guido,

this option is like making a copy of the business content, just like in BW, so it works perfectly for us.

Best regards,

Raúl.

entry.title: Re: Auto Release of Orders In Fiori
entry.content:
Hi @Sai_Nitin ,

I presume that you want to release orders belonging to a particular order type when created. You can do this in order type configuration (OIOA). By checking the 'release immediately' checkbox, the orders belonging to that particular order type will be released once created.


Regards,
Ritesh PR

entry.title: Re: SAP CAPM service failing with http 407 Proxy Authenti...
entry.content: Hi, we have/had the same problem. Have a look at the logs in the Cloud Connector, they may tell you that system parameters are missing. Nevertheless, we are still experiencing the problem, so we are now evaluating the Private Link Service.

entry.title: Re: WM putaway manual bin selection
entry.content: Nothing maintained in WH2 view. Movementtype customizing refers to SAP Standard. 311(IM) mapped to 312(WM). For 312 just TR/ZO creation is maintained. For my understanding there is no customizing to find a specific bin (besides fixed bin strat with assignment from MatMaster)

entry.title: How to restrict access to queues for specific clients in Advanced Event Mesh?
entry.content:
The documentation specifies how access to topics can be achieved through ACL's. But it is not clear how the same can be done for queues.

Is it possible to define that a client can only publish or consume messages from certain Queues? My understanding is that by default all clients can consume from all queues.

Example scenario of my requirements:

Client A should only be able to consume Queues X and Y

Client B should only be able to consume Queue Y

Client C should only be able to consume from Queues that follow a certain naming convention

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: WM putaway manual bin selection
entry.content: I am asking to check wm movement type 311 and 312 setting in activity define movement type see any bin is assigned except transfer

Please advise.

Thanks.

entry.title: SFC Card Core Plugin Extension Events Issue
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am working on core plugin extensions in SAP DM where in I am extending the SfcCardExtensionProvider  whenever there is change of resource status.

As per the github code for SfcCardExtensionProvider there are only four events mentioned in

PluginEventExtension.js.

I wanted to know are only those for events supported for SfcCardExtensionProvider or other events like ON_CLOSE_PLUGIN or ON_POD_SELECTION_CHANGE_EVENT are also supported by SfcCardExtensionProvider ?

I tried using other events like ON_CLOSE_PLUGIN or ON_POD_SELECTION_CHANGE_EVENT but the SfcCardExtensionProvider does not captures it whenever I changes the resource status through changeEquipmentStatus plugin and the plugin is closed.

Also, is there a way we can call plugin events of SfcCardExtensionProvider from any other extension plugin for example changeEquipmentStatus?

Reference Blog for plugin Extension 

Thanks and Regards,

Sabahat Shaikh

SAP Digital Manufacturing SAP Digital Manufacturing Insights @ManoelCosta

entry.title: Question About Contract Period Limits for Additional Systems in SAP Private Cloud
entry.content:
"Are there limits to the contract duration of an additional system in SAP Private Cloud?

For instance, I know that a Sandbox system can be purchased for a minimum period of 3 months, but is there a maximum duration? Does the customer need to commit to the additional system for the entire project duration, or is it possible to purchase an additional Sandbox system for, say, six months?

I would appreciate any insights or clarifications on this topic!"

entry.title: Re: Question regarding Data Source Integration in Content Form Templates
entry.content:
HI Karti,

You can query released CDS views via custom logic and populate custom fields in your custom form. Additionally, you can create a custom CDS view to store your custom status, query it at runtime and populate your custom form.

Is this what you were looking for?

Thanks.
Prasanth

entry.title: Issue with focus, scroll after SAPUI5 upgrade
entry.content:
Before SAPUI5 upgrade:
// handle tap for active toolbar, do nothing if already handled
Toolbar.prototype.ontap = function(oEvent) {
if (this.getActive() && !oEvent.isMarked()) {
oEvent.setMarked();
this.firePress({
srcControl : oEvent.srcControl
});
}
};

 

After upgrade of SAPUI5:
y.prototype.ontap = function(t) {
if (this.getActive() && !t.isMarked() || t.srcControl === this._getActiveButton()) {
t.setMarked();
this.firePress({
srcControl: t.srcControl
});
this.focus() ==> problem with this line as the scroll moves to initial position to the top.
}
}


My code:

var template= new sap.m.VBox({
      items: [
        new sap.m.Toolbar({ }),
        new sap.m.Panel({
          expand: [oController.expandPanel, oController],
          expandable: true,
          growing: true,
          headerToolbar: [
            new sap.m.OverflowToolbar({
              active: true,
              growing: true,
              press: [oController.panelSelect, oController],
              content: [
                new sap.m.Text({
                  layoutData: new sap.m.OverflowToolbarLayoutData({
                    priority: sap.m.OverflowToolbarPriority.NeverOverflow
                  }),
                  width: "20rem",
                  wrapping: false,
                  text: "{testModel>localName}",
                }),
                new sap.m.ToolbarSeparator()
               
    });

    var scrollRight1 = new sap.m.ScrollContainer("scrollright", {
      vertical: true,
      horizontal: false,
      height: "200rem",
      content: [new sap.m.VBox({
        id: "scroll",
        width: "100%",
        height: "50%",
      }).bindAggregation("items", "testModel>/danger", Template)]
    });

panelSelect: function (oEvent) {
     // content
    }
 
After press event above the scroll moves to initial position.
before UI5 upgrade everything works perfect with the same code. But after upgrade have an issue in scroll.
Could you please suggest.

The idea of the customer is not to have to archive all documents as attachments in the system and to use external storage instead.

Many thanks,

Stella

Any lead to address this will be of great help.

 

Thank you

Prasanna

entry.title: Aveniq convinces as pilot partner for new certification: SAP security operations
entry.content:
We appreciate the long-standing partnership with Aveniq, a trusted SAP-certified operations partner for more than ten years, with now four SAP-certified services. In discussions with Aveniq, they shared that security will be their key focus in the coming years. We were therefore delighted to have them as a pilot partner for the launch of our new security operations certification.

Before introducing a new certification, SAP selects partners to go through the certification process as pilots to ensure that the process runs smoothly and is optimally tailored to the subject area. With its extensive SAP security knowledge, Aveniq was ideally suited to be a pilot partner for the SAP security operations certification.

In an interview with Aveniq, we learn which services they offer in the area of SAP security for SAP Cloud customers. They also explain why they have defined security as a strategic focus area for themselves and the importance of this topic for their customers. They also outline why Aveniq decided to participate as a pilot partner for the new SAP Security Operations certification and what benefits they expect to gain from this certification.

 

May you want to start by telling us about your company and what services Aveniq is offering for SAP security to cloud customers?

Aveniq is a leading Swiss IT service provider with around 650 employees and is primarily active in the financial services, healthcare, industrial, utilities, public sector, and education sectors. Aveniq offers its customers innovative services for a modern workplace, hybrid/cloud solutions, SAP consulting and implementation, hyper-automation options, digitalization solutions as well as CAD/PLM solutions, and consulting services with a focus on IT infrastructure, IT security, and business continuity.

Aveniq offers SAP Cloud customers a wide range of security services:

Identity & Access Management: Management of users, roles, catalogs, SSO, provisioning, access control, and identity and access governance.

SAP Security Check & Monitoring: Checking and monitoring of SAP systems.

SAP Cloud Connector Security: Ensuring the security of the SAP Cloud Connector.

Support with internal and external audits: Help with the preparation and implementation of security audits.

Security awareness & training: training and awareness raising for security topics.

Code vulnerability analysis for SAP applications: analysis of security vulnerabilities in the code of SAP applications.

Attack surface reduction: measures to reduce the attack surface at application, and system level.

Data protection and security: protecting and ensuring privacy and data integrity.

EWA Security Findings: remediation of security findings from the Early Watch Alert.

Consulting: support in the implementation and optimization of SAP security strategies, from the analysis of security requirements to the development of security concepts and support during implementation.

 

What was the deciding factor for Aveniq to focus on security? And why is the topic so important for Aveniq and its customers? 

The importance of information security for long-term success and sustainability in a constantly changing, digital, and interconnected business world has increased massively. In the face of increasing cyber-attacks, a proactive, strategic approach to information security and data protection is essential. Aveniq has developed an end-to-end security strategy based on market needs and modern principles, encompassing both physical and digital security, management security, and operational security.

The topic of security is particularly important for several reasons:

Holistic approach: Aveniq emphasizes the importance of making no distinction between IT and OT, and taking a comprehensive approach that implements security, privacy, and protection across all levels according to the principle of “Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability” as per NIST CSF 2.0 and ZeroTrust. This includes systems, processes, products, information/data, people, and suppliers, i.e. the protection of company assets.

Improved security: Comprehensive, preventive security measures ensure a high level of protection that covers all attack vectors. This is supported by continuous monitoring and rapid response measures (response, recovery), which are ensured by our Security Operations Center (SOC).

Building trust: Compliance with international security standards and data protection principles, evidenced by various certifications such as ISO 27001, ISO 22301, and others, strengthens trust.

Adaptability: Flexibility and control in the hybrid and multi-cloud model enable specific customer needs to be met efficiently.

Increased efficiency: Consolidation of technologies and processes leads to improved infrastructure and application management and optimized costs for customers.

Customer-centric solutions: Modular and replicable services are designed to meet customers' individual requirements and business objectives, considering regulatory requirements and risk appetite.

This security orientation helps to strengthen the competitive position of customers and enables Aveniq to further expand its market position.

 

What factors influenced Aveniq's decision to support SAP as a pilot partner in the validation of the new certification 'SAP security operations'?

Since its initial certification as an SAP operations partner in 2011, Aveniq has continued to develop and adapt to changing customer needs and SAP's strategic direction. With a broad range of services - from SAP HANA operations services and various operating models such as RISE with SAP services to SAP remote support and SAP hosting in the Aveniq Cloud and via hyperscalers - we have been able to establish ourselves in the Swiss market with around 100 internal SAP specialists.

In 2018, we were one of the first partners in Switzerland to demonstrate our expertise in SAP S/4HANA solutions operations with a certification. Our certification for SAP BTP operations in 2024 illustrates our ongoing innovation and commitment to providing customers with advanced and secure SAP solutions. Our comprehensive knowledge of SAP security enables us to make a decisive contribution to the security and efficiency of our customers' SAP systems. Certification for SAP security operations was therefore the next logical step to put our expertise in this area to the test.

The fact that SAP has chosen us as a pilot partner for the new certification fills us with pride and gratitude. The joint pursuit of excellence is the cornerstone of our long-standing partnership as equals. We are delighted that the auditors were impressed by the quality of our SAP security services.

 

What benefits do you anticipate from being SAP-certified in SAP security operations for SAP cloud solutions?

We see several advantages in SAP certification for SAP security operations for SAP cloud solutions: 

  • The certification strengthens the customers’ confidence in Aveniq's security expertise and underlines the company's credibility as a trusted partner for SAP security solutions.
  • The certification ensures that Aveniq's SAP security operation services meet the highest standards and are continuously improved.
  • The certification enables Aveniq to offer its customers a higher level of protection and a better quality of service, resulting in higher customer satisfaction.
  • The certification strengthens the long-standing partnership between SAP and Aveniq - a connection that benefits both sides.

In summary, SAP certification not only strengthens a company's trust and credibility but also helps to optimize internal processes and open up new markets. It provides clear proof of compliance with standards and quality requirements, which is of great value to existing and potential customers as well as to the manufacturer itself.

 

 

 

Information on the certification program for operations partners and an overview of all certified partners can be found in the SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Visit OPC at SAP.com

entry.title: From FIORI LUNCHPAD, 500 Internet server errors occur when WDA tiles are opened.
entry.content:
Dear. All

An error occurs when you run FIORI and open the tiles on the spectacles mark.
500 Internet server error

If you open a regular tile without an eyeglasses mark, it will start without any problems.

There is no dump in ST22.
 

 

and a last question

 When I open the tile, the SAP screen appears, but I don't know how to solve it.

entry.title: Re: Add delay in Request message SAP PO
entry.content: Hi Ryan, we already have interfaces up and running in prod.. but this requirement is temporary where if sender sends a request that should not be forwarded to SAP backend.. .. instead should go to timeout or introduce delay in request so that the sender should not get any response.. but at the same time they should receive any error response.. this requirement is temporary and later we might need to remove this settings...for example if the channels are stopped then they would get error message 500 etc., what could be easy solution for such requirement? should we add timeout on adapter level? or introduce some delay in Request message mapping? or anything else which is easy and efficient?

entry.title: Re: Oportunidades!! Capacitação ABAP FIORI e FUNCIONAL SD
entry.content: Excelente! Realmente precisamos destas oportunidades!

entry.title: Re: Getting 403 forbidden error on accessing an OData Ser...
entry.content:
Hi!

Check in t-code SICF activation of 'zxx_Active_plant_srv' service.

 

Best regards,
Kostus

entry.title: Re: Embracing Sustainability with SAP Ariba
entry.content: Very good Abhijit, lean new things & new concepts

entry.title: Re: WM putaway manual bin selection
entry.content:
Hi 

Please IMG(SPRO) Logistic Execution-Warehouse Management- Activity- Transfer- Define Movement Type

Check your warehouse number and movement type 311 312  click on that 

see 

and share your screen shot. 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Re: Adding Variables to Predictive Analysis
entry.content: Hi, in your example, how would you add an entire month, such as September? Regarding granularity, would you choose "Month-Year" or just "Year"? This is a question I have since I'm relatively new to forecasting, and I can't seem to find information on how to create a proper module for forecasting.

entry.title: Companion content not visible in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hello everybody,

We've encountered an issue with our SAP Companion content on SuccessFactors.

Our existing content (guided tours, context help) has disappeard from both the SF Homepage and Employee Central.

When clicking on the question mark icon, an empty carousel appears. Additionally, if I attempt to create new content in edit mode, I receive an error message indicating that the creation has failed.

Here’s what we’ve tried so far:

  • Verified the context of the content in the Producer, which seems to match (see attachments).
  • Republished the content, but this did not resolve the issue.

Interestingly, the Companion content on SF Learning is working fine. However, the UI for Learning was recently upgraded, so the content there is newer. Could this UI upgrade have caused issues with our older content?

If not, are there any other areas we should investigate?

Thank you in advance for your support!

Joke

entry.title: What is the difference between SOAP RM and SOAP 1. in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to the difference between soap rm and soap 1.x. can anyone please explain this and How can we distinguish which adapter has to use in the iflow?

I have seen some blogs not able to understand. I want this in the perspective of SAP CPI.

Thanks,

Upendra.
